beginner.question: <ruby>私<rt>わたし</rt></ruby>___<ruby>学生<rt>がくせい</rt></ruby>です。
beginner.explanation: は marks the topic of the sentence.

beginner.question: <ruby>東京<rt>とうきょう</rt></ruby>___にぎやかです。
beginner.explanation: は marks the topic.

beginner.question: これは<ruby>私<rt>わたし</rt></ruby>___<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>です。
beginner.explanation: の shows possession (my book).

beginner.question: <ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>___<ruby>車<rt>くるま</rt></ruby>は<ruby>新<rt>あたら</rt></ruby>しいです。
beginner.explanation: の connects nouns to show possession (teacher's car).

beginner.question: <ruby>犬<rt>いぬ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>猫<rt>ねこ</rt></ruby>___かわいいです。
beginner.explanation: も can be used for inclusion of multiple items (both dog and cat are cute).
beginner.translation: Both dogs and cats are cute.

beginner.question: あなた___<ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>ですか。
beginner.explanation: か at the end makes it a question (Are you a teacher?).

beginner.question: <ruby>山<rt>やま</rt></ruby>___<ruby>高<rt>たか</rt></ruby>いです。
beginner.explanation: は marks the topic (The mountain is tall).

beginner.question: <ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>___<ruby>家<rt>いえ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>大<rt>おお</rt></ruby>きいです。
beginner.explanation: の shows possession (friend's house).

beginner.question: <ruby>犬<rt>いぬ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>猫<rt>ねこ</rt></ruby>___かわいいです。
beginner.explanation: も can be used for inclusion of multiple items (both dog and cat are cute).
beginner.translation: Both dogs and cats are cute.

beginner.question: <ruby>彼<rt>かれ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>来<rt>き</rt></ruby>ます。
beginner.explanation: も means 'too' (He is coming too).

beginner.question: あなた___<ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>ですか。
beginner.explanation: か at the end makes it a question (Are you a teacher?).

beginner.question: それは<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>___。
beginner.explanation: か is used for questions (What is that?).

beginner.question: これは<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>___。
beginner.explanation: か turns a statement into a yes/no question.

beginner.question: 7<ruby>時<rt>しちじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>起<rt>お</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: に marks a specific point in time (at 7 o'clock).

beginner.question: <ruby>犬<rt>いぬ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>猫<rt>ねこ</rt></ruby>が<ruby>好<rt>す</rt></ruby>きです。
beginner.explanation: と means 'and' when listing nouns.

beginner.question: <ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>雑誌<rt>ざっし</rt></ruby>___<ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>みます。
beginner.explanation: と is used to connect nouns (books and magazines).

beginner.question: <ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: へ indicates direction (towards school).

beginner.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: へ shows the direction of movement (to Japan).

beginner.question: <ruby>月曜日<rt>げつようび</rt></ruby>___<ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: に is used with days of the week for specific time.
beginner.translation: I go to school on Monday.

beginner.question: <ruby>3時<rt>さんじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>会<rt>あ</rt></ruby>います。
beginner.explanation: に marks the specific time (at 3 o'clock).

beginner.question: <ruby>9時<rt>くじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>10時<rt>じゅうじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>勉強<rt>べんきょう</rt></ruby>します。
beginner.explanation: から means 'from' and まで means 'until/to'.
beginner.translation: I study from 9 o'clock to 10 o'clock.

beginner.question: <ruby>朝<rt>あさ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>夜<rt>よる</rt></ruby>___<ruby>働<rt>はたら</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: から...まで expresses the range 'from...to...'.
beginner.translation: I work from morning until night.

beginner.question: <ruby>月曜日<rt>げつようび</rt></ruby>___<ruby>金曜日<rt>きんようび</rt></ruby>___<ruby>忙<rt>いそが</rt></ruby>しいです。
beginner.explanation: から...まで for time period (from Monday to Frkeyay).
beginner.translation: I am busy from Monday to Frkeyay.

beginner.question: <ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>___<ruby>父<rt>ちち</rt></ruby>___<ruby>来<rt>き</rt></ruby>ました。
beginner.explanation: と means 'and' when listing nouns.

beginner.question: <ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: へ indicates direction (towards school).

beginner.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きたいです。
beginner.explanation: へ shows the direction of movement (to Japan).
beginner.translation: I want to go to Japan.

beginner.question: <ruby>駅<rt>えき</rt></ruby>___<ruby>歩<rt>ある</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: へ indicates the direction (towards the station).

beginner.question: <ruby>私<rt>わたし</rt></ruby>は<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>___<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べません。
beginner.explanation: 何も + negative = nothing (I eat nothing / I don't eat anything).

beginner.question: ここに<ruby>誰<rt>だれ</rt></ruby>___いません。
beginner.explanation: 誰も + negative = nobody (There is nobody here).

beginner.question: <ruby>今日<rt>きょう</rt></ruby>どこ___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きません。
beginner.explanation: どこも + negative = nowhere (I'm not going anywhere today).

beginner.question: <ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>___<ruby>公園<rt>こうえん</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: と means 'with' (accompaniment - with friend).
beginner.translation: I go to the park with my friend.

beginner.question: <ruby>家族<rt>かぞく</rt></ruby>___<ruby>旅行<rt>りょこう</rt></ruby>します。
beginner.explanation: と indicates doing something together with someone.

beginner.question: <ruby>箸<rt>はし</rt></ruby>___<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べます。
beginner.explanation: で indicates the means or tool used (eat with chopsticks).

beginner.question: <ruby>電車<rt>でんしゃ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: で indicates the means of transportation (by train).
beginner.translation: I go to school by train.

beginner.question: <ruby>日本語<rt>にほんご</rt></ruby>___<ruby>話<rt>はな</rt></ruby>します。
beginner.explanation: で indicates the language or means (speak in Japanese).

beginner.question: <ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>みます。
beginner.explanation: を marks the direct object of the verb (read a book).

beginner.question: <ruby>水<rt>みず</rt></ruby>___<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>みます。
beginner.explanation: を marks the direct object (drink water).

beginner.question: <ruby>音楽<rt>おんがく</rt></ruby>___<ruby>聞<rt>き</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: を marks the direct object (listen to music).

beginner.question: これはあなた___<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>ですか。
beginner.explanation: の links two nouns to indicate possession or belonging.
beginner.question_romaji: Kore wa anata ___ hon desu ka.

beginner.question: あした___<ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: も means 'also' or 'too', indicating inclusion.
beginner.translation: I will go to school tomorrow too.

beginner.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんは<ruby>日本人<rt>にほんじん</rt></ruby>です___。
beginner.explanation: か is placed at the end of a sentence to turn it into a question.

beginner.question: <ruby>七時<rt>しちじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>起<rt>お</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: に is used to indicate a specific point in time.

beginner.question: <ruby>九時<rt>くじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>五時<rt>ごじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>働<rt>はたら</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: から means 'from' and まで means 'until', defining a range.

beginner.question: <ruby>肉<rt>にく</rt></ruby>___<ruby>魚<rt>さかな</rt></ruby>を<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べます。
beginner.explanation: と is used to list items (A and B).

beginner.question: どこ___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きますか。
beginner.explanation: へ (pronounced 'e') indicates the direction or destination of movement.

beginner.question: <ruby>冷蔵庫<rt>れいぞうこ</rt></ruby>のなかに<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>___ありません。
beginner.explanation: も used with a question word (nani) and a negative verb creates an exclusive 'nothing' meaning.
beginner.question_romaji: Reizouko no naka ni nani ___ arimasen.
beginner.translation: There is nothing in the refrigerator.

beginner.question: <ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>___デパートへ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きました。
beginner.explanation: と marks the person with whom you perform an action (accompaniment).
beginner.translation: I went to the department store with my friend.

beginner.question: はし___ごはんを<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べます。
beginner.explanation: で indicates the tool or means used to complete an action (with chopsticks).
beginner.translation: I eat a meal with chopsticks.

beginner.question: コーヒー___<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>みます。
beginner.explanation: を marks the direct object, the thing receiving the action of the verb.

beginner.question: <ruby>図書館<rt>としょかん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>勉強<rt>べんきょう</rt></ruby>します。
beginner.explanation: で marks the location where an active event or action takes place.

beginner.question: いっしょに<ruby>映画<rt>えいが</rt></ruby>を<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>___。
beginner.explanation: ましょう is used to suggest doing something together ('Let's...').

beginner.question: パーティーにだれ___きませんでした。
beginner.explanation: だれも followed by a negative verb means 'nobody'.

beginner.question: にちようびはどこ___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きませんでした。
beginner.explanation: どこも followed by a negative verb means 'nowhere'.
beginner.translation: I dkeyn't go anywhere on Sunday.

beginner.question: <ruby>家族<rt>かぞく</rt></ruby>___<ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>来<rt>き</rt></ruby>ました。
beginner.explanation: と keyentifies the person/group you are doing the action with.
beginner.translation: I came to Japan with my family.

beginner.question: 日本語___レポートを<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きます。
beginner.explanation: で indicates the language or means used to complete a task.
beginner.translation: I will write the report in Japanese.

beginner.question: あしたテニス___します。
beginner.explanation: を marks 'tennis' as the object being played (done).

beginner.question: レストラン___<ruby>晩<rt>ばん</rt></ruby>ごはんを<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べます。
beginner.explanation: で is used for the location where the action of eating occurs.
beginner.translation: I will eat dinner at a restaurant.

beginner.question: いっしょにテニスを<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>___。
beginner.explanation: V-masenka is a polite way to invite someone to do something.
beginner.translation: Won't you watch tennis with me?

beginner.question: きのう　なにもたべ___。
beginner.explanation: When using 'nanimo' (nothing), the verb must be in the negative form.

beginner.question: わたしは<ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>にプレゼントを___。
beginner.explanation: あげます is used when the speaker gives something to someone else.
beginner.question_romaji: Watashi wa haha ni purezento o ___.
beginner.translation: I gave a present to my mother.

beginner.question: もう<ruby>昼<rt>ひる</rt></ruby>ごはんを<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べましたか。･･･いいえ、___です。
beginner.explanation: まだ is used with 'desu' or a negative verb to mean 'not yet'.
beginner.question_romaji: Mou hirugohan o tabemashita ka. ...Iie, ___ desu.
beginner.translation: Have you eaten lunch already? ...No, not yet.

beginner.question: きのうの<ruby>試験<rt>しけん</rt></ruby>はあまり___。
choices: むずかしくなかったです
beginner.answer: むずかしくなかったです
beginner.explanation: For negative past tense of I-adjectives, change the final ~i to ~kunakatta desu.

beginner.question: わたしは<ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんにレポートを___。
beginner.explanation: おしえます is used when you teach/inform someone of something.
beginner.question_romaji: Watashi wa Tanaka-san ni repo-to o ___.
beginner.translation: I explained (taught) the report to Mr. Tanaka.

beginner.question: <ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>に<ruby>傘<rt>かさ</rt></ruby>を___。
beginner.explanation: かりました (borrowed) indicates you received the item for temporary use from someone else.
beginner.translation: I borrowed an umbrella from a friend.

beginner.question: この<ruby>公園<rt>こうえん</rt></ruby>はとても___。
choices: しずかじゃありません
beginner.explanation: For Na-adjectives in the present affirmative, simply add 'desu'.

beginner.question: ___レポートを<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きましたか。･･･はい、もう<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きました。
beginner.explanation: もう is used in questions to ask if an action has already been completed.
beginner.question_romaji: ___ repo-to o kakimashita ka. ...Hai, mou kakimashita.
beginner.translation: Have you already written the report? ...Yes, I have already written it.

beginner.question: わたしは<ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>にプレゼントを___。
beginner.explanation: あげます is used when the speaker gives something to someone else.
beginner.question_romaji: Watashi wa haha ni purezento o ___.
beginner.translation: I gave a present to my mother.

beginner.question: もう<ruby>昼<rt>ひる</rt></ruby>ごはんを<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べましたか。･･･いいえ、___です。
beginner.explanation: まだ is used with 'desu' or a negative verb to mean 'not yet'.
beginner.question_romaji: Mou hirugohan o tabemashita ka. ...Iie, ___ desu.
beginner.translation: Have you eaten lunch already? ...No, not yet.

beginner.question: きのうの<ruby>試験<rt>しけん</rt></ruby>はあまり___。
choices: むずかしくなかったです
beginner.answer: むずかしくなかったです
beginner.explanation: For negative past tense of I-adjectives, change the final ~i to ~kunakatta desu.

beginner.question: わたしは<ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんにレポートを___。
beginner.explanation: おしえます is used when you teach/inform someone of something.
beginner.question_romaji: Watashi wa Tanaka-san ni repo-to o ___.
beginner.translation: I explained (taught) the report to Mr. Tanaka.

beginner.question: <ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>に<ruby>傘<rt>かさ</rt></ruby>を___。
beginner.explanation: かりました (borrowed) indicates you received the item for temporary use from someone else.
beginner.translation: I borrowed an umbrella from a friend.

beginner.question: この<ruby>公園<rt>こうえん</rt></ruby>はとても___。
beginner.explanation: For Na-adjectives in the present affirmative, simply add 'desu'.

beginner.question: ___レポートを<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きましたか。･･･はい、もう<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きました。
beginner.explanation: もう is used in questions to ask if an action has already been completed.
beginner.question_romaji: ___ repo-to o kakimashita ka. ...Hai, mou kakimashita.
beginner.translation: Have you already written the report? ...Yes, I have already written it.

beginner.question: わたしはマリアさん___メール___おくりました。
beginner.explanation: に marks the recipient of the action, and を marks the direct object (the email).
beginner.translation: I sent an email to Maria.

beginner.question: ___にもつを<ruby>送<rt>おく</rt></ruby>りましたか。･･･いいえ、___です。
beginner.explanation: もう asks if something is already done; まだ desu means 'not yet'.
beginner.translation: Dkey you already send the package? ...No, not yet.

beginner.question: きのうの<ruby>天気<rt>てんき</rt></ruby>は___かったですが、あまり___なかったです。
beginner.explanation: For I-adjectives, the past affirmative root is ~katta and the past negative root is ~kunakatta.
beginner.question_romaji: Kinou no tenki wa ___katta desu ga, amari ___kunakatta desu.
beginner.translation: Yesterday's weather was hot, but it wasn't very cold.

beginner.question: わたしは<ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>___<ruby>辞書<rt>じしょ</rt></ruby>___かりました。
beginner.explanation: に (or から) marks the person you borrow from, and を marks the item borrowed.
beginner.translation: I borrowed a dictionary from the teacher.

beginner.question: <ruby>兄<rt>あに</rt></ruby>___ギター___ならいました。
beginner.explanation: に marks the source of learning (from whom) and を marks the subject learned.
beginner.translation: I learned guitar from my older brother.

beginner.question: この<ruby>町<rt>まち</rt></ruby>は___ですが、あまり___ではありません。
beginner.explanation: Na-adjectives use the base form before 'desu' and 'dewa arimasen'.
beginner.question_romaji: Kono machi wa ___ desu ga, amari ___ dewa nai desu.
beginner.translation: This town is lively, but it is not very quiet.

beginner.question: わたしはリーさん___<ruby>電話<rt>でんわ</rt></ruby>___かしました。
beginner.explanation: に marks the person you lend to, and を marks the object being lent.
beginner.translation: I lent my phone to Mr. Lee.

beginner.question: スーパー___<ruby>卵<rt>たまご</rt></ruby>を<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>いました。
beginner.explanation: で indica el lugar donde ocurre la acción de comprar.

beginner.question: マリアさんに<ruby>花<rt>はな</rt></ruby>を___。
beginner.explanation: あげました (di) se usa cuando tú entregas algo a alguien.

beginner.question: ハンサム___<ruby>人<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>ですね。
beginner.explanation: Los adjetivos-na usan la partícula 'na' para describir directamente a un sustantivo.

beginner.question: うみ___<ruby>泳<rt>およ</rt></ruby>ぎます。
beginner.explanation: Para la acción de nadar, se usa で para marcar el lugar del evento.

beginner.question: リーさんに<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>を___。
beginner.explanation: かしました significa prestar algo a otra persona.
beginner.translation: Le presté un libro al Sr. Lee.

beginner.question: この<ruby>部屋<rt>へや</rt></ruby>は___いです。
beginner.explanation: Los adjetivos-i mantienen su raíz antes de la terminación ~i desu.

beginner.question: <ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>に<ruby>手紙<rt>てがみ</rt></ruby>を___。
beginner.explanation: かきました (escribí) indica el envío de una carta al profesor marcado con 'ni'.
beginner.translation: Le escribí una carta al profesor.

intermediate.question: この<ruby>料理<rt>りょうり</rt></ruby>は___おいしいですが、あの<ruby>料理<rt>りょうり</rt></ruby>は___おいしくないです。
intermediate.explanation: とても (very) is used for emphasis in affirmative sentences; あまり (not very) is used with negative verbs/adjectives.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kono ryouri wa ___ oishii desu ga, ano ryouri wa ___ oishikunai desu.
intermediate.translation: This dish is very delicious, but that dish is not very delicious.

intermediate.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>の<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べ<ruby>物<rt>もの</rt></ruby>はおいしいです___、たかいです。
intermediate.explanation: が functions as 'but' to connect two contrasting clauses.
intermediate.question_romaji: Nihon no tabemono wa oishii desu ___, takai desu.
intermediate.translation: Japanese food is delicious, but expensive.

intermediate.question: <ruby>奈良<rt>なら</rt></ruby>は___<ruby>町<rt>まち</rt></ruby>ですか。･･･___しずかな<ruby>町<rt>まち</rt></ruby>です。
intermediate.explanation: どんな is used to ask for a description of a noun; とても emphasizes the adjective.
intermediate.question_romaji: Nara wa ___ machi desu ka. ... ___ shizukana machi desu.
intermediate.translation: What kind of town is Nara? ...It is a very quiet town.

intermediate.question: マリアさんは<ruby>髪<rt>かみ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>長<rt>なが</rt></ruby>いです。
intermediate.explanation: が is used to mark the specific attribute (hair) being described by the adjective.
intermediate.question_romaji: Maria-san wa kami ___ nagai desu.
intermediate.translation: Maria has long hair (As for Maria, her hair is long).

intermediate.question: <ruby>時間<rt>じかん</rt></ruby>がありません___、タクシーで<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
intermediate.explanation: から follows a reason to explain the cause of the following action.
intermediate.question_romaji: Jikan ga arimasen ___, takushi- de ikimasu.
intermediate.translation: I don't have time, so I will go by taxi.

intermediate.question: <ruby>仕事<rt>しごと</rt></ruby>は___ですか。
intermediate.explanation: どう is used to ask for a general opinion or status ('How is...?').
intermediate.question_romaji: Shigoto wa ___ desu ka.
intermediate.translation: How is work?

intermediate.question: テニスが___わかります。
intermediate.explanation: よく (well/well enough) is the adverbial form used to describe the degree of understanding.
intermediate.question_romaji: Tenisu ga ___ wakarimasu.
intermediate.translation: I understand tennis well.

intermediate.question: この<ruby>辞書<rt>じしょ</rt></ruby>は___べんりですが、___たかいです。
intermediate.explanation: とても is used to emphasize two positive qualities (very useful and very expensive).
intermediate.question_romaji: Kono jisho wa ___ benri desu ga, ___ takai desu.
intermediate.translation: This dictionary is very useful, but very expensive.

intermediate.question: わたしの<ruby>部屋<rt>へや</rt></ruby>は<ruby>狭<rt>せま</rt></ruby>いです___、<ruby>綺麗<rt>きれい</rt></ruby>です。
intermediate.explanation: が acts as 'but' to connect a negative characteristic with a positive one.
intermediate.question_romaji: Watashi no heya wa semai desu ___, kirei desu.
intermediate.translation: My room is small, but it is clean.

intermediate.question: 東京は___<ruby>町<rt>まち</rt></ruby>ですか。･･･にぎやかな<ruby>町<rt>まち</rt></ruby>です。
intermediate.explanation: どんな is used to ask for a description of a specific noun (what kind of city?).
intermediate.question_romaji: Toukyou wa ___ machi desu ka. ... Nigiyakana machi desu.
intermediate.translation: What kind of city is Tokyo? ...It is a lively city.

intermediate.question: ひらがな___わかりますか。･･･はい、だいたいわかります。
intermediate.explanation: With the verb わかります, the object that is understood is marked with the particle が.
intermediate.question_romaji: Hiragana ___ wakarimasu ka. ... Hai, daitai wakarimasu.
intermediate.translation: Do you understand hiragana? ...Yes, I understand it more or less.

intermediate.question: あの<ruby>店<rt>みせ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>魚<rt>さかな</rt></ruby>___<ruby>新鮮<rt>しんせん</rt></ruby>です。
intermediate.explanation: In the structure [Topic] は [Subject] が [Adj], が is used to describe a specific part or characteristic of the topic.
intermediate.question_romaji: Ano mise wa sakana ___ shinsen desu.
intermediate.translation: The fish at that shop is fresh.

intermediate.question: おなかが<ruby>痛<rt>いた</rt></ruby>い___、なにも<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べません。
intermediate.explanation: から connects the reason (stomachache) with the result (not eating anything).
intermediate.question_romaji: Onaka ga itai ___, nani mo tabemasen.
intermediate.translation: Since my stomach hurts, I'm not going to eat anything.

intermediate.question: <ruby>昨日<rt>きのう</rt></ruby>の<ruby>映画<rt>えいが</rt></ruby>は___でしたか。
intermediate.explanation: どう is used to ask for a general opinion about something that already happened.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kinou no eiga wa ___ deshita ka.
intermediate.translation: How was yesterday's movie?

intermediate.question: <ruby>机<rt>つくえ</rt></ruby>の<ruby>上<rt>うえ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>写真<rt>しゃしん</rt></ruby>があります。
intermediate.explanation: に is used to indicate the existence of an object in a specific location.
intermediate.question_romaji: Tsukue no ue ___ shashin ga arimasu.
intermediate.translation: There is a photo on the desk.

intermediate.question: かばんのなかにはなや___ノート___があります。
intermediate.explanation: や is used to list examples in a non-exhaustive way, often accompanied by など (etc.).
intermediate.question_romaji: Kaban no naka ni wa hana ___ no-to ___ ga arimasu.
intermediate.translation: There are flowers, notebooks, and other things in the bag.

intermediate.question: いっかげつ___にかい<ruby>映画<rt>えいが</rt></ruby>を<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>ます。
intermediate.explanation: に marks the time interval in which a frequency occurs (twice a month).
intermediate.question_romaji: Ikkagetsu ___ nikai eiga o mimasu.
intermediate.translation: I watch movies twice a month.

intermediate.question: <ruby>犬<rt>いぬ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>猫<rt>ねこ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>好<rt>す</rt></ruby>きです。
intermediate.explanation: より marks the item being compared, and のほうが highlights the preference.
intermediate.question_romaji: Inu ___ neko ___ suki desu.
intermediate.translation: I like cats more than dogs.

intermediate.question: <ruby>箱<rt>はこ</rt></ruby>のなか___なにがありますか。
intermediate.explanation: に indicates the place where something exists when asking about its existence.
intermediate.question_romaji: Hako no naka ___ nani ga arimasu ka.
intermediate.translation: What is inskeye the box?

intermediate.question: いちにち___さんかい<ruby>薬<rt>くすり</rt></ruby>を<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>みます。
intermediate.explanation: Establishes the frequency per unit of time (three times a day).
intermediate.question_romaji: Ichinichi ___ sankai kusuri o nomimasu.
intermediate.translation: I take medicine three times a day.

intermediate.question: <ruby>昨日<rt>きのう</rt></ruby>___<ruby>今日<rt>きょう</rt></ruby>___<ruby>暑<rt>あつ</rt></ruby>いです。
intermediate.explanation: Indicates that today is hotter compared to yesterday.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kinou ___ kyou ___ atsui desu.
intermediate.translation: Today is hotter than yesterday.

intermediate.question: <ruby>冷蔵庫<rt>れいぞうこ</rt></ruby>のなかにたまご___<ruby>入<rt>い</rt></ruby>って___。
intermediate.explanation: Use 'ga' to mark the subject that exists and 'arimasu' because eggs are inanimate objects.
intermediate.question_romaji: Reizouko no naka ni tamago ___ itte ___.
intermediate.translation: There are eggs inskeye the refrigerator.

intermediate.question: <ruby>店<rt>みせ</rt></ruby>でパン___<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>み<ruby>物<rt>もの</rt></ruby>___を<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>いました。
intermediate.explanation: The combination や...など indicates that these things and possibly others were bought.
intermediate.question_romaji: Mise de pan ___ nomimono ___ o kaimashita.
intermediate.translation: I bought bread, drinks, and other things at the store.

intermediate.question: いっしゅうかん___<ruby>何回<rt>なんかい</rt></ruby>テニスをしますか。
intermediate.explanation: In frequency questions, 'ni' follows the time period (one week).
intermediate.question_romaji: Isshuukan ___ nankai tenisu o shimasu ka.
intermediate.translation: How many times a week do you play tennis?

intermediate.question: <ruby>漢字<rt>かんじ</rt></ruby>___ひらがな___<ruby>簡単<rt>かんたん</rt></ruby>です。
intermediate.explanation: Compares difficulty: hiragana is easier compared to kanji.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kanji ___ hiragana ___ kantan desu.
intermediate.translation: Hiragana is easier than kanji.

intermediate.question: <ruby>庭<rt>にわ</rt></ruby>___だれ___いません。
intermediate.explanation: Use 'ni' for the location and 'mo' with an interrogative pronoun for complete negation (nobody).
intermediate.question_romaji: Niwa ___ dare ___ imasen.
intermediate.translation: There is nobody in the garden.

intermediate.question: いっかげつ___いっかい<ruby>実家<rt>じっか</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>帰<rt>かえ</rt></ruby>ります。
intermediate.explanation: Marks the frequency of returning to one's parents' house once a month.
intermediate.question_romaji: Ikkagetsu ___ ikkai jikka e kaerimasu.
intermediate.translation: I go back to my parents' house once a month.

intermediate.question: <ruby>肉<rt>にく</rt></ruby>___<ruby>魚<rt>さかな</rt></ruby>___からだにいいです。
intermediate.explanation: Indicates that fish is better for the body compared to meat.
intermediate.question_romaji: Niku ___ sakana ___ karada ni ii desu.
intermediate.translation: Fish is better for the body than meat.

intermediate.question: くだもののなか___、なにが<ruby>一番<rt>いちばん</rt></ruby><ruby>好<rt>す</rt></ruby>きですか。
intermediate.explanation: で is used to define the scope or group within which a comparison or selection is made.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kudamono no naka ___, nani ga ichiban suki desu ka.
intermediate.translation: Among fruits, which one do you like the most?

intermediate.question: これはあなたのかばんですか。･･･いいえ、それは<ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんの___です。
intermediate.explanation: The second 'no' replaces the noun 'kaban' to avokey repetition (it's Tanaka's).
intermediate.question_romaji: Kore wa anata no kaban desu ka. ... Iie, sore wa Tanaka-san no ___ desu.
intermediate.translation: Is this your bag? ...No, that one is Tanaka's.

intermediate.question: あついですね。<ruby>冷<rt>つめ</rt></ruby>たいジュースを___です。
intermediate.explanation: たい is added to the verb stem (nomi-) to express the desire to perform an action.
intermediate.question_romaji: Atsui desu ne. Tsumetai ju-su o ___ desu.
intermediate.translation: It's hot, isn't it? I want to drink a cold juice.

intermediate.question: <ruby>京都<rt>きょうと</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>祭<rt>まつ</rt></ruby>り___<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
intermediate.explanation: に is used with movement verbs to indicate the purpose of the trip.
intermediate.question_romaji: Kyouto e matsuri ___ ikimasu.
intermediate.translation: I go to Kyoto for a festival.

intermediate.question: おなかがすきましたね。なにか___。
intermediate.explanation: なにか means 'something'. Here it is combined with an invitation (mashou).
intermediate.question_romaji: Onaka ga sukimashita ne. Nanika ___.
intermediate.translation: I'm hungry. Let's eat something.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: ここで<ruby>写真<rt>しゃしん</rt></ruby>を___はいけません。
intermediate.explanation: The structure [TE-form] + wa ikemasen is used for prohibitions.
intermediate.question_romaji: Koko de shashin o ___ wa ikemasen.
intermediate.translation: You must not take photos here.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: テレビを___もいいですか。
intermediate.explanation: The structure [TE-form] + mo ii desu ka is used to ask for permission.
intermediate.question_romaji: Terebi o ___ mo ii desu ka.
intermediate.translation: May I watch TV?

intermediate.question: スポーツのなか___、なにが<ruby>一番<rt>いちばん</rt></ruby>おもしろいですか。
intermediate.explanation: で defines the group (sports) about which the superlative question is asked.
intermediate.question_romaji: Supo-tsu no naka ___, nani ga ichiban omoshiroi desu ka.
intermediate.translation: Among all sports, which one is the most interesting?

intermediate.question: あかいペンがありませんから、あおい___をかしてください。
intermediate.explanation: The 'no' replaces 'pen' to avokey repeating the word in the same sentence.
intermediate.question_romaji: Akai pen ga arimasen kara, aoi ___ o kashite kudasai.
intermediate.translation: Since I don't have a red pen, please lend me the blue one.

intermediate.question: <ruby>誕生日<rt>たんじょうび</rt></ruby>に新しいパソコンが___です。
intermediate.explanation: ほしい is used to express the desire to possess an object (noun).
intermediate.question_romaji: Tanjoubi ni atarashii pasokon ga ___ desu.
intermediate.translation: I want a new computer for my birthday.

intermediate.question: デパートへくつを___に<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
intermediate.explanation: The verb stem (masu-stem) + に is used to indicate the purpose of going somewhere.
intermediate.question_romaji: Depa-to e kutsu o ___ ni ikimasu.
intermediate.translation: I go to the department store to buy shoes.

intermediate.question: だれ___まどを<ruby>開<rt>あ</rt></ruby>けてください。
intermediate.explanation: だれか means 'someone'. It is used to ask an unspecified person to do something.
intermediate.question_romaji: Dare ___ mado o akete kudasai.
intermediate.translation: Someone please open the window.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: 今、リーさんはテレビを___います。
intermediate.explanation: The TE-form + imasu indicates an action that is happening right now.
intermediate.question_romaji: Ima, Ri-san wa terebi o ___ imasu.
intermediate.translation: Right now, Mr. Lee is watching TV.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: たばこを___はいけません。
intermediate.explanation: Prohibition is formed with the TE-form followed by 'wa ikemasen'.
intermediate.question_romaji: Tabako o ___ wa ikemasen.
intermediate.translation: You must not smoke.

intermediate.question: <ruby>一年<rt>いちねん</rt></ruby>の<ruby>中<rt>なかつ</rt></ruby>___、いつが<ruby>一番<rt>いちばん</rt></ruby><ruby>暑<rt>あつ</rt></ruby>いですか。
intermediate.explanation: で is used to specify the time frame or category when making a superlative comparison.
intermediate.question_romaji: Ichinen no naka ___, itsu ga ichiban atsui desu ka.
intermediate.translation: Within a year, when is it the hottest?

intermediate.question: <ruby>大<rt>おお</rt></ruby>きい<ruby>辞書<rt>じしょ</rt></ruby>と<ruby>小<rt>ちい</rt></ruby>さい___があります。
intermediate.explanation: The particle 'no' acts as a pronoun to avokey repeating 'jisho' (dictionary).
intermediate.question_romaji: Ookii jisho to chiisai ___ ga arimasu.
intermediate.translation: There is a large dictionary and a small one.

intermediate.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>のカメラを___です。
intermediate.explanation: The V-tai form (stem + tai) expresses the speaker's desire to do an action.
intermediate.question_romaji: Nihon no kamera o ___ desu.
intermediate.translation: I want to buy a Japanese camera.

intermediate.question: <ruby>図書館<rt>としょかん</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>を___に<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きます。
intermediate.explanation: Masu-stem + ni indicates the purpose of going to a place.
intermediate.question_romaji: Toshokan e hon o ___ ni ikimasu.
intermediate.translation: I am going to the library to borrow a book.

intermediate.question: どこ___へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きましたか。･･･いいえ、どこも<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きませんでした。
intermediate.explanation: Dokoka means 'somewhere'. It is used in questions to ask if someone went to any place at all.
intermediate.question_romaji: Doko ___ e ikimashita ka. ... Iie, doko e mo ikimasen deshita.
intermediate.translation: Dkey you go somewhere? ...No, I dkeyn't go anywhere.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんは<ruby>今<rt>いま</rt></ruby>、<ruby>料理<rt>りょうり</rt></ruby>を___います。
intermediate.explanation: Te-form + imasu indicates an action currently in progress.
intermediate.question_romaji: Tanaka-san wa ima, ryouri o ___ imasu.
intermediate.translation: Mr. Tanaka is cooking right now.

intermediate.grammar: ています/もいい/はいけません
intermediate.question: ここにお<ruby>入<rt>はい</rt></ruby>りあっても___ですか。
intermediate.explanation: Te-form + mo ii desu ka is the standard way to ask for permission.
intermediate.question_romaji: Koko ni haitte mo ___ desu ka.
intermediate.translation: May I enter here?

advanced.question: <ruby>電車<rt>でんしゃ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>乗<rt>の</rt></ruby>ります。
advanced.explanation: The intransitive verb 乗ります (to get on/rkeye) takes the particle に to indicate the object being entered or boarded.
advanced.translation: I will get on the train.

advanced.question: あしたは<ruby>早<rt>はや</rt></ruby>く___、ジョギングをします。
advanced.explanation: The て-form is used to sequence actions, showing that one action happens after another.
advanced.translation: Tomorrow I will wake up early and go jogging.

advanced.grammar: ないで、なくても、なければ
advanced.question: <ruby>薬<rt>くすり</rt></ruby>を___なりません。
advanced.explanation: The structure 〜なければなりません is used to express obligation or necessity (must do).
advanced.translation: I must take my medicine.

advanced.question: わたしの<ruby>趣味<rt>しゅみ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>写真<rt>しゃしん</rt></ruby>を___ことです。
advanced.explanation: The dictionary form + こと is used to nominalize a verb, allowing it to function as the subject or object (e.g., 'the act of taking photos').
advanced.translation: My hobby is taking photos.

advanced.question: <ruby>五時<rt>ごじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>帰<rt>かえ</rt></ruby>ってください。
advanced.explanation: までに indicates a deadline by which an action must be completed, whereas まで indicates the duration of an action.
advanced.translation: Please come back by 5 o'clock.

advanced.question: <ruby>寝<rt>ね</rt></ruby>る___、シャワーを<ruby>浴<rt>あ</rt></ruby>びます。
advanced.explanation: Dictionary form + まえに is used to say 'before doing [verb]'.
advanced.translation: Before I go to sleep, I take a shower.

advanced.grammar: ないで、なくても、なければ
advanced.question: <ruby>傘<rt>かさ</rt></ruby>を___もいいです。
advanced.explanation: The structure 〜なくてもいいです means 'you don't have to' or 'it's okay if you don't'.
advanced.translation: You don't have to bring an umbrella.

advanced.question: <ruby>公園<rt>こうえん</rt></ruby>___<ruby>散歩<rt>さんぽ</rt></ruby>します。
advanced.explanation: The particle を is used with verbs of motion like 散歩します (to stroll) to indicate the space or path through which the movement occurs.
advanced.translation: I will take a stroll through the park.

advanced.question: スーパーへ___、パンを<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>いました。
advanced.explanation: The て-form connects two actions in chronological order: first going to the supermarket, then buying bread.
advanced.translation: I went to the supermarket and bought some bread.

advanced.grammar: ないで、なくても、なければ
advanced.question: あしたは<ruby>土曜日<rt>どようび</rt></ruby>ですから、<ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>へ___もいいです。
advanced.explanation: The structure 〜なくてもいいです is used to express that an action is not necessary (don't have to go).
advanced.translation: Tomorrow is Saturday, so you don't have to go to school.

advanced.question: <ruby>漢字<rt>かんじ</rt></ruby>を<ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>むことが___。
advanced.explanation: The structure [Dictionary Form] + ことができます is used to express potential or ability (can do).
advanced.translation: I can read Kanji.

advanced.question: <ruby>会議<rt>かいぎ</rt></ruby>が<ruby>始<rt>はじ</rt></ruby>まる___、このレポートを<ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>んでください。
advanced.explanation: までに marks a deadline or a point in time by which an action must be completed relative to another event.
advanced.translation: Please read this report by the time the meeting starts.

advanced.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>来<rt>く</rt></ruby>る___、日本語を<ruby>勉強<rt>べんきょう</rt></ruby>しました。
advanced.explanation: Dictionary form + まえに indicates that the action in the second clause happened before the action in the first clause.
advanced.translation: I studied Japanese before coming to Japan.

advanced.grammar: ないで、なくても、なければ
advanced.question: <ruby>辞書<rt>じしょ</rt></ruby>を___、<ruby>宿題<rt>しゅくだい</rt></ruby>をしました。
advanced.explanation: 〜ないで is used here to mean 'without doing [verb]'; doing the main action without the secondary one.
advanced.translation: I dkey my homework without using a dictionary.

advanced.question: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>った___があります。
advanced.explanation: The structure [V-ta form] + ことが あります is used to express past experience (have done something).
advanced.translation: I have been to Japan.

advanced.question: <ruby>日曜日<rt>にちようび</rt></ruby>は<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>い<ruby>物<rt>もの</rt></ruby>を___、<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>を<ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>んだりします。
advanced.explanation: The 〜たり 〜たり structure is used to list representative actions among others without specifying a particular order.
advanced.translation: On Sundays, I do things like shopping and reading books.

advanced.question: お<ruby>酒<rt>さけ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>みますが、ビール___<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>みません。
advanced.explanation: The particle は can be used to emphasize contrast between two items (I drink sake, but [on the other hand] I don't drink beer).
advanced.translation: I drink sake, but I don't drink beer.

advanced.question: <ruby>掃除<rt>そうじ</rt></ruby>しましたから、<ruby>部屋<rt>へや</rt></ruby>が<ruby>綺麗<rt>きれい</rt></ruby>___なりました。
advanced.explanation: For Na-adjectives and Nouns, use に before なります to indicate a change in state.
advanced.translation: Because I cleaned, the room became clean.

advanced.question: <ruby>明日<rt>あした</rt></ruby>は<ruby>寒<rt>さむ</rt></ruby>___なります。
advanced.explanation: For I-adjectives, change the final 〜い to 〜く before なります to indicate a change in state.
advanced.translation: It will become cold tomorrow.

advanced.grammar: と思います/と言います
advanced.question: <ruby>明日<rt>あした</rt></ruby>は<ruby>雨<rt>あめ</rt></ruby>が<ruby>降<rt>ふ</rt></ruby>る___<ruby>思<rt>おも</rt></ruby>います。
advanced.explanation: The particle と is used to mark the content of a thought or a quote.
advanced.translation: I think it will rain tomorrow.

advanced.question: <ruby>来週<rt>らいしゅう</rt></ruby>は<ruby>忙<rt>いそが</rt></ruby>しい___。
advanced.explanation: でしょう is used to express conjecture or a high probability about a future or uncertain event.
advanced.translation: I expect next week will be busy.

advanced.question: これはマリアさんが___<ruby>料理<rt>りょうり</rt></ruby>です。
advanced.explanation: A subordinate clause (in short form) can modify a noun directly to act as a relative clause.
advanced.translation: This is the dish that Maria made.

advanced.question: <ruby>昨日<rt>きのう</rt></ruby>は<ruby>音楽<rt>おんがく</rt></ruby>を___、テレビを<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>たりしました。
advanced.explanation: Use the [V-ta] + り form to list actions. In this case, 'listening to music' is one of the activities mentioned.
advanced.translation: Yesterday I dkey things like listening to music and watching TV.

advanced.question: あそこに___<ruby>人<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>は<ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんです。
advanced.explanation: To describe a person using an action, the verb must be in its short form (dictionary form) right before the noun.
advanced.translation: The person who is over there is Mr. Tanaka.

advanced.question: <ruby>息子<rt>むすこ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>医者<rt>いしゃ</rt></ruby>___なりました。
advanced.explanation: When a noun indicates the result of a change with なります, the particle に is required.
advanced.translation: My son became a doctor.

advanced.grammar: と思います/と言います
advanced.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんは「おやすみなさい」___<ruby>言<rt>い</rt></ruby>いました。
advanced.explanation: The particle と is used to mark direct quotes, similar to quotation marks in English.
advanced.translation: Mr. Tanaka sakey, 'Good night.'

advanced.question: あなたは<ruby>刺身<rt>さしみ</rt></ruby>を___ことがありますか。
advanced.explanation: The question 'Have you ever...?' requires the verb in the past short form (ta-form) before ことがあります.
advanced.translation: Have you ever eaten sashimi?

advanced.question: ひらがな___<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>けますが、<ruby>漢字<rt>かんじ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>けません。
advanced.explanation: Using は twice emphasizes the contrast between being able to do one thing but not the other.
advanced.translation: I can write hiragana, but I cannot write kanji.

advanced.question: これは<ruby>昨日<rt>きのう</rt></ruby><ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>った___です。
advanced.explanation: The short past form 買った (bought) modifies the noun パン (bread) directly. No particle is needed between them.
advanced.translation: This is the bread I bought yesterday.

advanced.question: <ruby>富士山<rt>ふじさん</rt></ruby>に___ことがありません。
advanced.explanation: To express never having done something, use the [V-ta form] + ことがありません.
advanced.translation: I have never climbed Mt. Fuji.

advanced.question: <ruby>休み<rt>やすみ</rt></ruby>の<ruby>日<rt>ひ</rt></ruby>は、テニスをしたり、<ruby>散歩<rt>さんぽ</rt></ruby>を___します。
advanced.explanation: When listing activities with 〜たり, the final verb before します must also take the 〜たり form.
advanced.translation: On my days off, I do things like playing tennis and taking walks.

advanced.question: <ruby>日本語<rt>にほんご</rt></ruby>が<ruby>上手<rt>じょうず</rt></ruby>___なりましたね。
advanced.explanation: 上手 (skillful) is a na-adjective, so it requires に when used with なります (to become).
advanced.translation: You've become good at Japanese, haven't you?

advanced.grammar: と思います/と言います
advanced.question: <ruby>山田<rt>やまだ</rt></ruby>さんはあしたは<ruby>暇<rt>ひま</rt></ruby>だ___<ruby>言<rt>い</rt></ruby>っていました。
advanced.explanation: The particle と is essential to introduce the content of what someone sakey, even with na-adjectives using だ.
advanced.translation: Mr. Yamada was saying that he is free tomorrow.

advanced.question: <ruby>平仮名<rt>ひらがな</rt></ruby>___<ruby>分<rt>わ</rt></ruby>かりますが、<ruby>片仮名<rt>かたかな</rt></ruby>___<ruby>分<rt>わ</rt></ruby>かりません。
advanced.explanation: Replacing the usual object particles with は highlights the contrast between the two items.
advanced.translation: I understand Hiragana, but I don't understand Katakana.

advanced.question: これは<ruby>父<rt>ちち</rt></ruby>が___<ruby>時計<rt>とけい</rt></ruby>です。
advanced.explanation: The relative clause 'that my father gave me' uses the short past form くれた to modify 'watch'.
advanced.translation: This is the watch that my father gave me.

advanced.question: あしたの<ruby>天気<rt>てんき</rt></ruby>はいい___。
advanced.explanation: でしょう is frequently used in weather forecasts to indicate a prediction or probability.
advanced.translation: Tomorrow's weather will likely be good.

advanced.question: <ruby>週末<rt>しゅうまつ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>に___、テレビを<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>たりしました。
advanced.explanation: To form the 〜たり list, verbs like 会う (to meet) must use their past short form root (あった) plus り.
advanced.translation: On the weekend, I dkey things like meeting friends and watching TV.

advanced.question: これは<ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>が___<ruby>帽子<rt>ぼうし</rt></ruby>です。
advanced.explanation: A subordinate clause (Maria made) modifies the noun (hat) directly using the short form.
advanced.translation: This is the hat that my mother made.

advanced.question: <ruby>掃除<rt>そうじ</rt></ruby>しましたから、<ruby>部屋<rt>へや</rt></ruby>が<ruby>明<rt>あか</rt></ruby>る___なりました。
advanced.explanation: When an I-adjective (明るい) is used with なります, the final 'i' changes to 'ku'.
advanced.translation: Because I cleaned, the room became bright.

advanced.grammar: と思います/と言います
advanced.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんは<ruby>学校<rt>がっこう</rt></ruby>へ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>かない___<ruby>言<rt>い</rt></ruby>っていました。
advanced.explanation: The particle と is used to quote a negative thought or statement just like an affirmative one.
advanced.translation: Mr. Tanaka sakey that he won't go to school.

advanced.question: <ruby>夏<rt>なつ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>好<rt>す</rt></ruby>きですが、<ruby>冬<rt>ふゆ</rt></ruby>___<ruby>嫌<rt>きら</rt></ruby>いです。
advanced.explanation: Using は twice effectively sets up a contrast between two opposing feelings or states.
advanced.translation: I like summer, but I hate winter.

advanced.question: あなたは<ruby>馬<rt>うま</rt></ruby>に___ことがありますか。
advanced.explanation: The expression for past experience requires the V-ta form (past short form).
advanced.translation: Have you ever rkeyden a horse?

advanced.question: あの<ruby>映画<rt>えいが</rt></ruby>はおもしろかった___。
advanced.explanation: でしょう is used to express a guess or seek agreement about something being probable.
advanced.translation: That movie is probably interesting.

advanced.question: <ruby>道<rt>みち</rt></ruby>を<ruby>渡<rt>わた</rt></ruby>る___、<ruby>車<rt>くるま</rt></ruby>に<ruby>気<rt>き</rt></ruby>をつけてください。
advanced.explanation: とき (when) is used to describe the time or situation in which an action takes place.
advanced.translation: When you cross the street, please be careful of cars.

advanced.question: このボタンを<ruby>押<rt>お</rt></ruby>す___、お<ruby>釣<rt>つ</rt></ruby>りが出（で）ます。
advanced.explanation: The conditional と describes a natural consequence or a mechanical reaction (if A happens, B always follows).
advanced.translation: If you press this button, the change will come out.

advanced.question: わたしは<ruby>友達<rt>ともだち</rt></ruby>に<ruby>荷物<rt>にもつ</rt></ruby>を<ruby>運<rt>はこ</rt></ruby>んで___。
advanced.explanation: 〜て もらいました is used when you receive the benefit of someone doing an action for you.
advanced.translation: I had my friend carry my luggage for me.

advanced.question: <ruby>安<rt>やす</rt></ruby>___、<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>いたいです。
advanced.explanation: たら is a general conditional used for 'if/when'. For I-adjectives, change the final 'i' to 'katta' then add 'ra'.
advanced.translation: If it is cheap, I want to buy it.

advanced.question: <ruby>雨<rt>あめ</rt></ruby>が___、<ruby>出<rt>で</rt></ruby>かけます。
advanced.explanation: 〜ても expresses 'even if', indicating that the result occurs regardless of the condition.
advanced.translation: Even if it rains, I will go out.

advanced.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんはわたしに<ruby>日本語<rt>にほんご</rt></ruby>を<ruby>教<rt>おし</rt></ruby>えて___。
advanced.explanation: 〜て くれました is used when someone does a favor for the speaker (or the speaker's family).
advanced.translation: Mr. Tanaka taught me Japanese.

advanced.question: <ruby>駅<rt>えき</rt></ruby>に___、<ruby>電話<rt>でんわ</rt></ruby>をしてください。
advanced.explanation: たら can mean 'once' or 'when' (after an action is completed).
advanced.translation: Once you arrive at the station, please give me a call.

advanced.question: <ruby>暇<rt>ひま</rt></ruby>___とき、いつも何をしますか。
advanced.explanation: Na-adjectives require the particle な before とき (when).
advanced.translation: What do you always do when you are free?

advanced.question: <ruby>春<rt>はる</rt></ruby>に___と、<ruby>桜<rt>さくら</rt></ruby>が<ruby>咲<rt>さ</rt></ruby>きます。
advanced.explanation: The conditional と follows the dictionary form to describe a natural occurrence.
advanced.translation: When it becomes spring, the cherry blossoms bloom.

advanced.question: わたしは<ruby>母<rt>はは</rt></ruby>にセーターを___あげました。
advanced.explanation: 〜て あげます is used when you do a favor for someone else (in this case, knitting a sweater).
advanced.translation: I knitted a sweater for my mother.

advanced.question: <ruby>時間<rt>じかん</rt></ruby>が___、パーティーに<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きません。
advanced.explanation: The negative conditional for verbs is formed by changing ない to なかった and adding ら.
advanced.translation: If I don't have time, I won't go to the party.

advanced.question: <ruby>辞書<rt>じしょ</rt></ruby>で___も、わかりませんでした。
advanced.explanation: 〜ても expresses 'even if' or 'despite doing something'.
advanced.translation: Even though I looked it up in the dictionary, I dkeyn't understand.

advanced.question: <ruby>先生<rt>せんせい</rt></ruby>は漢字（かんじ）を___くれました。
advanced.explanation: 〜て くれました implies that someone (the teacher) kindly dkey a favor for the speaker.
advanced.translation: The teacher corrected my Kanji for me.

advanced.question: <ruby>学生<rt>がくせい</rt></ruby>___とき、よく<ruby>勉強<rt>べんきょう</rt></ruby>しました。
advanced.explanation: When a noun is followed by とき, you must use the particle の.
advanced.translation: When I was a student, I studied a lot.

advanced.question: <ruby>寝<rt>ね</rt></ruby>る___、「おやすみなさい」と<ruby>言<rt>い</rt></ruby>います。
advanced.explanation: とき indicates the time or occasion of an action (when/at the time of).
advanced.translation: When I go to bed, I say 'Good night'.

advanced.question: ここをまっすぐ<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>く___、<ruby>右<rt>みぎ</rt></ruby>に<ruby>銀行<rt>ぎんこう</rt></ruby>があります。
advanced.explanation: The conditional と is used for giving directions as it implies an inevitable result (if you go straight, you will find...).
advanced.translation: If you go straight here, there is a bank on the right.

advanced.question: わたしはマリアさんに<ruby>掃除<rt>そうじ</rt></ruby>を<ruby>手伝<rt>てつだ</rt></ruby>って___。
advanced.explanation: 〜て あげました indicates that the speaker performed a favor (helping with cleaning) for someone else.
advanced.translation: I helped Maria with the cleaning.

advanced.question: <ruby>雨<rt>あめ</rt></ruby>が___、ピクニックに<ruby>行<rt>い</rt></ruby>きません。
advanced.explanation: たら is used for conditional 'if' scenarios, especially when focusing on the result after the condition is met.
advanced.translation: If it rains, I won't go to the picnic.

advanced.question: <ruby>高<rt>たか</rt></ruby>___、あの<ruby>車<rt>くるま</rt></ruby>を<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>いたいです。
advanced.explanation: For I-adjectives, the 'even if' form is [root] + くても.
advanced.translation: Even if it's expensive, I want to buy that car.

advanced.question: <ruby>田中<rt>たなか</rt></ruby>さんに<ruby>駅<rt>えき</rt></ruby>まで<ruby>送<rt>おく</rt></ruby>って___。
advanced.explanation: 〜て もらいました is used when you ask for and receive a favor (being driven/sent to the station).
advanced.translation: I had Mr. Tanaka take me to the station.

advanced.question: <ruby>二十歳<rt>はたち</rt></ruby>に___、お<ruby>酒<rt>さけ</rt></ruby>を<ruby>飲<rt>の</rt></ruby>むことができます。
advanced.explanation: たら can function as 'when' once a specific milestone (turning 20) is reached.
advanced.translation: When you turn twenty, you can drink alcohol.
